name: Early termination fee
summary: Fees charged for ending a contract (cellular, fitness, gym, fiber) before its stated term.

evidence.claim: Cellular ETFs were largely eliminated by the major US carriers (Verizon, T-Mobile, AT&T) by 2018 due to switch-incentive programs; however, device-financing ETFs still apply for unpaid phone balances.

body.description: Contract fee for cancelling before the stated term ends. Common in gym memberships ($50-$500), fiber/cable contracts ($100-$400 declining), and device-financing plans (remaining balance, which can be $500+). Traditional cellular ETFs mostly eliminated (E1).

disclosureLegality.requirement: Must be disclosed at contract signing per FTC. Excessive ETFs have been ruled unconscionable by some state courts.

negotiability.script: I need to cancel my service. I understand you have an ETF, but [reason for cancellation — moving, job loss, deployment, etc.]. Can you waive or prorate the ETF as a loyalty retention?
